chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI SCRA 191 of 2019 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection No. 25. 2. For hearing of main case. 01.10.2025 This reference is pending since 2019 without any progress and even notice has not been sought / issued till date. On 24.04.2019, following order was passed: “25-04-2019 Mr. Abdul Latif Chandio, advocate for applicant. 1. Granted. 2. Learned counsel for the applicant undertakes to comply with office objections before the next date of hearing. 3. Granted, subject to all just exceptions. 4. Instant reference application appears to have been filed against order dated 26.2.22019, passed by the Customs Appellate Tribunal on the rectification application in Customs Appeal No.H-99/2018, which appeal was finally decided vide judgment dated 20.11.20018, whereas, the questions of law proposed through instant reference application appears to relate to the judgment in Customs Appeal No.H-99/2018, and do not arise from the order passed on the rectification application. This court has been pleased to hold that no question of law arises against the order passed on the rectification application except one question i.e. “Whether the Appellate Tribunal was justified to allow / dismiss the rectification application”. Learned counsel for the applicant, while confronted with hereinabove position, requested for time to assist the Court. At his request, adjourned to a date to be fixed after three weeks.” Thereafter the applicant remained unrepresented without intimation or justification.

Learned counsel for the applicant, while confronted with hereinabove position, requested for time to assist the Court. At his request, adjourned to a date to be fixed after three weeks.” Thereafter the applicant remained unrepresented without intimation or justification. Same is the case today. It appears that the applicant is not interested in present proceedings. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, this reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Customs Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 196 of the Customs Act, 1969. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
